mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving equity prices up over the short-to-medium term.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS shows no significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a certain threshold signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, which negatively impacts equity valuations over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Recessionary periods often see sharp increases in unemployment.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with strong housing starts indicate an accommodative monetary policy supporting a recovering demand sector, which boosts investor confidence and drives equity prices higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 crisis, low rates and housing recovery were key.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and HOUST 3-month average growth exceeds 5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, accelerating industrial production indicates strong business activity and increasing corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident indicator of economic cycles.
failure_condition: If, during confirmed economic expansion periods, a 3-month annualized growth in INDPRO above 2% does not lead to positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) below a certain level, especially after a period of high inflation, signals disinflationary pressures that could lead to more accommodative monetary policy, boosting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Central banks often react to disinflation with rate cuts or dovish guidance.
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is negative, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving equity prices higher over the very short term.
failure_condition: If the month-over-month growth rate of PAYEMS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and declining unemployment indicates a robust economic expansion with increasing demand and full employment, which is highly favorable for corporate profits and equity markets over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 3% and UNRATE 3-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Strong GDP and falling unemployment are hallmarks of bull markets.
failure_condition: If, when GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth exceeds 3% and UNRATE is falling, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank to curb price pressures, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical periods of high inflation and rising rates have been challenging for equities.
failure_condition: If, during periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 4% YoY) and rising FEDFUNDS, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of observations.
